label: 543_fema_emergency management_federal emergency_agency fema
description: Congress considers bills modifying FEMA's Individuals and Households Program, allowing repurposing of funds, and authorizing crisis counseling. These measures aim to enhance disaster relief effectiveness and administrative capacity.

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 2903, FEMA Reauthorization Act of 2012
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure on March 8, 2012 H.R. 2903 would authorize appropriations totaling about $2.2 billion over the next two years for the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). The legislation would authorize about $2.1 billion for salaries and expenses…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 501, Promoting Resilient Buildings Act of 2025
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 501 would authorize the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to establish a pilot program and award grants to state and tribal governments designed to mitigate the effects of future disasters on residential properties by reducing damage from flooding, wind, wildfire, and seismic activity. Under the bill,…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 1491, Disaster Related Extension of Deadlines Act
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 1491 would amend the Internal Revenue Code to modify the statute of limitations on filing for a tax credit or tax refund in the case of declared disasters. The bill would treat any filing extension granted in such cases as applicable to the allowable period for filing a return to receive a tax credit or tax…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 517, Filing Relief for Natural Disasters Act
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 517 would amend the Internal Revenue Code to modify the rules for postponing certain tax deadlines in response to declared disasters. The bill would permit the Secretary of the Treasury, in the case of certain state-declared disasters, to grant filing extensions that are similar to those granted in the event of…

central_articles.title: S. 5067, Disaster Survivors Fairness Act of 2024
central_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the Senate Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs on September 25, 2024 On This Page Bill Summary S. 5067 would modify the Individuals and Households Program (IHP) of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA), which assists households following a disaster. central_articles.title: H.R. 744, Disaster Management Costs Modernization Act
central_articles.excerpt: H.R. 744 would allow state and local governments that receive disaster assistance from the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to repurpose unused funds that were originally allocated for management costs to increase their administrative capacity to prepare for, recover from, or mitigate the effects of future…

central_articles.title: S. 3677, Post-Disaster Mental Health Response Act
central_articles.excerpt: S. 3677 would authorize the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to cover the costs of crisis counseling for people affected by emergencies declared under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act when those services are paid for by local governments. Under current law, such federal…
